research.title: Transactional Email as React Components: React Email + Resend
research.whyTrending: React Email + Resend became the default 2026 stack for developer-friendly transactional email; entirely uncovered on the blog, highly practical, strong SEO for a common real-world task.

seo.suggestedTitle: Transactional Email in Next.js: React Email + Resend

gaps: Setup guides skip WHY email HTML is weird (tables, inline styles, Outlook Word renderer) - which is the whole reason React Email exists
Deliverability (SPF/DKIM/DMARC) treated as an afterthought when its the difference between inbox and spam
Gmail 102KB clipping and plain-text version rarely mentioned
Idempotency / not double-sending on retries not covered
strengths: React Email preview server nice DX

expertInsights: 2026 standard stack: React Email (build templates as components) + Resend (delivery API)
Email HTML is stuck in the past: clients strip <style> so inline styles only; layout via tables not flex/grid (Outlook uses Word renderer); keep width <=600px
Gmail clips emails over 102KB behind view entire message; include a plain-text version
React Email renders components to email-safe HTML across 50+ clients; local preview server for DX
Send from a server action/route with the Resend SDK; dev can send to your own address without domain verification
Production requires domain verification: TXT (SPF) + CNAME/TXT (DKIM), plus DMARC - the inbox-vs-spam difference
Resend webhooks POST delivery/open/click/bounce/complaint events to an endpoint you configure
commonProblems: Landing in spam because SPF/DKIM/DMARC not set up
Double-sending on server action retries (no idempotency)
Using flex/grid layout that breaks in Outlook
No plain-text alternative -> deliverability + a11y hit

strategy.angle: A production email guide that leads with WHY email is different (its HTML from 1999 - tables, inline styles, Outlook Word engine) which is exactly the problem React Email solves, then covers the send path via server action, and spends real space on the two things that separate a demo from production: deliverability (SPF/DKIM/DMARC) and reliability (idempotency, webhooks, suppression)
strategy.title: Transactional Email in Next.js: React Email + Resend Done Right
strategy.excerpt: Email HTML is stuck in 1999 - inline styles, table layouts, and Outlook rendering with Word. React Email hides that; Resend delivers it. Heres the full stack for Next.js: components as templates, sending from a server action, and the deliverability and reliability work that keeps you out of spam.
hooks: Contrarian: the reason to use React Email isnt DX, its that raw email HTML is a hostile 1999 environment you dont want to hand-write
Gotcha: your beautiful flexbox email renders as a broken mess in Outlook, which still uses the Word engine
Practical: the demo sends an email; production is SPF/DKIM/DMARC, idempotency, and bounce webhooks
outline: Hook: the email that looked perfect except in Outlook
Why email HTML is its own hostile world
React Email: components that compile to email-safe HTML
Sending from a Next.js server action with Resend
Deliverability: SPF, DKIM, DMARC (inbox vs spam)
Webhooks: bounces, complaints, and the suppression list
The gotchas: Gmail clipping, plain text, 600px
